skill.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skill.description: Guide AI assistants to split independent coding tasks across parallel agents, then review and integrate their results.
skill.summary: Dispatch focused agents for independent debugging or implementation work.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences and ordinary planning text as command execution, cryptography, and reconnaissance. Context review found documentation examples only, with no executable scripts, network access, secret handling, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are documentation fences for DOT, TypeScript, Markdown, and plain text examples. They do not define scripts or run external commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are Markdown fence markers or example task text. No executable file, shell invocation, or user input command path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: Line 3 contains the skill description about independent tasks. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, key material, or encryption operation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain frontmatter prose. It contains no crypto-related code or security-sensitive operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Patterns Are Benign Workflow Text
low_findings.description: Line 60 describes an agent goal, and line 169 advises checking for edit conflicts. No network scan, host discovery, system inventory, or data collection is requested.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines are normal coordination guidance. The skill does not instruct agents to enumerate systems, probe networks, or extract environment data.

content.value_statement: Independent coding failures waste time when one assistant investigates them sequentially. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code divide tasks, run agents in parallel, and integrate results.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when coding tasks are independent enough for parallel work.
Shows how to scope one agent per failing test file or subsystem.
Provides prompt structure for focused debugging assignments.
Explains how to review returned summaries before integration.
Lists cases where parallel dispatch should not be used.
limitations: Does not provide executable orchestration code or agent APIs.
Requires the host assistant to support parallel task delegation.
Does not resolve merge conflicts or validate code changes automatically.
Works best when task boundaries are already clear.

use_cases.title: Split Independent Test Failures
use_cases.description: Assign separate agents to unrelated failing test files, then compare fixes before running the full suite.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Multi-Area Debugging
use_cases.description: Send focused investigations to different subsystems when defects have separate causes and no shared state.

use_cases.title: Teach Agent Delegation Patterns
use_cases.description: Use the examples to train teams on safe scoping, constraints, and review steps for AI agents.

prompt_templates.title: Find Parallel Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these failures and group them by independent problem domain. Tell me which groups can be assigned to separate agents.

prompt_templates.title: Create Agent Tasks
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare scoped prompts for multiple agents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create one focused agent task for each independent failing area. Include scope, goal, constraints, and expected return summary.

prompt_templates.title: Review Agent Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these agent summaries. Identify conflicts, missing verification, and the safest order for integrating the changes.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Parallel Debugging Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a larger refactor regression.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a parallel debugging plan for these regressions. Define task boundaries, agent prompts, integration checks, and full-suite verification steps.

output_examples.input: Three unrelated test files fail after a refactor.
output_examples.output: A triage plan with one agent per test file, clear constraints, and a final integration checklist.

output_examples.input: Two agents return fixes that touch nearby behavior.
output_examples.output: A review summary that highlights possible overlap, asks for targeted verification, and recommends full test execution.

output_examples.input: A team wants rules for when not to parallelize agent work.
output_examples.output: A short decision guide covering related failures, shared state, broad discovery, and files that agents may edit together.

best_practices: Give each agent one narrow problem domain with enough context to work alone.
State edit constraints clearly, especially when agents must avoid shared files.
Review every returned summary before combining changes and running full verification.
anti_patterns: Do not dispatch agents when one fix could affect all failures.
Do not ask multiple agents to edit the same files without coordination.
Do not accept parallel results without checking conflicts and running tests.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It helps you decide when to split independent coding tasks across multiple AI agents.

faq.question: Does it run agents by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow and prompt patterns for tools that already support agent delegation.

faq.question: When should I avoid this skill?
faq.answer: Avoid it when failures share state, need broad discovery, or may be fixed by one root cause.

faq.question: Can it help with test failures?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples focus on grouping unrelated failing test files into separate investigations.

faq.question: How should I review agent outputs?
faq.answer: Read each summary, check for overlapping edits, run targeted tests, then run the full suite.

faq.question: Is this useful outside Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance applies to Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and similar agent-capable coding tools.
